name: Newlander, Bertha
motto: "She with all the charm of woman, She with all the breadth of man."
description: She came from far-off Sweden to the burg of Grafton, whence she came as a graduate of the High School to the University. She is an earnest, persistent worker, successful in every undertaking. Our Only Girl.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Baker, George Everett
motto: "He doth indeed show some sparks that are like wit."
description: His terrestrial existence began in Erie County, New York. He received his early education in the city of his adoption, Emerado, whence his thirst for a higher education brought him to the University, years ago. Our Dutchman.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Besse, J. Raoul
motto: "After death, the doctor."
description: Raoul first saw light in Crookston, Minnesota, but claims to be a direct descendant of the House of Bourbon—and has been a connoisseur of that brand ever since. He came to the University in 1898, and intends to pursue the study of medicine at the McGill University, Montreal. His specialty is languages and his forte is Spanish. Our Nobleman.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Craig, Russel
motto: "All the world loves a lover."
description: He, too, originated in Ontario, but came West to seek fortune and happiness. By persistent effort he has gained his place among us, and has laid a broad foundation for his future study of medicine. By his own hands he has hewn his bark out of the toughened timbers that grow by the road to Success. Sure, may its sailing be. Our Un-confirmed Bachelor.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Herbert, Goodall
motto: "Has sighed to many, though he loved but one."
description: He is our lone product of Dakota soil, and came to us from Bathgate as a preparatory student. A man of many qualities, he is a favorite with all, but especially popular with the fairer sex. He has, indeed, done more than any other one member of his class to join the faculty with the student body in bonds of sympathy and admiration. Our Ladies' Man.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Hilborn, Ernest C.
motto: "Cheered by himself with ends of verse and sayings of philosophers."
description: He began his earthly career in Appleton, Wisconsin, in the same year as Mr. Tanner. Westward he came in 1890, and, graduating from the Valley City Normal in 1903, he came to the University the same year. Our Busy Bee.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Hutchinson, William H.
motto: "The world knows only two, that's Rome and I."
description: He donned these "mortal coils" in Alexandria, Minnesota, whence he removed at an early age to La Moure County. Graduating from the Valley City Normal in 1903, he came the same year to the University. Our Handsome Man.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Lykken, Henry G.
motto: "A mighty hunter, and his prey is woman. Go in to kill."
clubs: Business Manager and Editor, Weekly Student
description: Henry G. came to the "U" with the determination to win an education and a wife, and has succeeded in the former. He was born in Dakota County, Minnesota, when the state was young and came West on the trail of the Mormon migration. His only regret is that he can save but one old maid. Our Viking.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_02.png

name: John, McLean
motto: "Much may be made, even of a Scotchman, when caught young."
description: He was born in Fisher, Minnesota, but forsook his native soil for the fertile plains of a newer state. John is an enthusiastic student of his chosen profession and sees in every clod of earth, a hidden treasure. 'Tis said of him, he spoiled an able preacher to make a miner. Our Orator.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Thomas, George Pierson
motto: "My wife is a constellation of virtues; she's the moon, and I am the man in the moon."
description: He comes from the home of the Blarney stone, gifted with its powers. To North Dakota he came in 1890 and, a few years later, entered the Valley City Normal, where he won oratorical distinction and a wife. He came to us as a graduate of that institution in 1903. Our Married Man.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_03.png

name: William, R. Robinson
motto: "I am no orator as Brutus is. But as you know me all, a plain, blunt man."
description: He was born in Ontario, but came when still young to the state of his adoption. Regarding his early education, little is known; but he began his sojourn at the University in the preparatory department and has remained faithful throughout. Our Athlete.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Cherry, J. Rose
motto: "On their own merits modest men are dumb."
description: As nearly as can be determined, he originated in Red Lake Falls, Minnesota. To us, however, he came as a graduate of the Grand Forks High School. He is an exceptionally strong student and will be a credit to his profession. Like his classmate, Bill, he makes little pretensions to oratory and insists that a forensic accomplishment beyond the apt use of adjectives has little place in the shop. Our Shortest Man.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Sprague, Gilbert
motto: "To insure or not to insure, That is the question."
description: He came to us as a graduate of the Grand Forks High School, and is a man of business worth, energetic and tactful. Insurance is his vocation. Our Business Man.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Tanner, Jesse A.
motto: "Whence is thy learning? Hath thy toil O'er books consumed the midnight oil?"
description: A debater, a scholar and an earnest worker, he was born when quite young in Mercer County, Pennsylvania, where he received his early education. Following Horace Greeley's advice, he came West and entered the "U" in 1903 as a graduate of the Valley City Normal. Our Conservative.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Wilcox, Lee Lane
motto: "The man that calls you Tom or Jack, And proves by thumping on your back."
description: He is originally from New York, but graduated from the University Arts course in 1904, winning honors as a debater, orator, athlete, and a successful student. A strong student in his chosen profession, he is bound to succeed. Our Daddy, That's All.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Ward, Innis
motto: "If he be not a fellow with the best king, thou shalt find the best king of good fellows."
description: Innis is a New Yorker by birth and of Celtic descent, but strong in his Teutonic affinity. He graduated from the Arts Department in '04, but his still unquenched thirst for learning drove him again to the Fount of Knowledge. Failing in his attempt to enter the Training School, he returned once more to the "U." It is hoped that his two degrees may gain him a hearing when next he tries. Our Joker.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Muriel, Glaspell
motto: "Such heart was in her, being then A little prattler among men."
description: She was born in Canada, that country whence so many good people hail. She came to Grafton in 1888. She attended the public school and the high school in Grafton, graduating from the latter in the class of '03. She entered the University in the fall of the same year and joined the Normal class. She is a very good member of Adelphi but her heart is with Ad Altiora.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Holmes, Vivian A.
motto: "'Tis pleasant, sure, to see one's name in print; A book's a book, although there's nothing in't."
description: Miss Holmes was born in Grand Forks, has lived in Grand Forks and has attended the schools of Grand Forks. She entered the University in the fall of 1903. She has kept at her work in a sure and steady way without flurry or worry. Being a good observer, she has noticed the disappointments which are apt to follow rashness and she manages her affairs accordingly.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Kristinson, Arni
motto: "That man that hath a tongue, I say, is no man, If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."
description: Mr. Kristinson, born in Iceland, is a descendant of the sturdy Vikings. He came to this country in 1888 and has since been "knocked about, sometimes in Canada, sometimes here" in North Dakota. He entered the University in 1899 and would have graduated with the class of '04 had he not stayed away from school last year. He is the Y. M. C. A. correspondent for the Weekly Student. He has always been a hard and diligent student.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Lokken, O. J.
motto: "While chastening thoughts of sweetest use bestowed By wisdom, moralize his pensive road."
description: He was born in Norway, came to the United States, attended the common schools of North Dakota, studied at Willmar Seminary and Business College, kept school, worked on the farm, and entered the University, all in the Nineteenth century. He is noted for two things: first, for never having taken a girl to a party because, as he says, he doesn't want to show partiality; and, second, for nothing.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_05.png

name: McClintock, Gertrude
motto: "A lady fair she is, highly esteemed by Men but more by Boys."
clubs: Participant in the Main declamation contest
Class correspondent for the Weekly Student
description: She is both a North Dakota born and a North Dakota bred lady. She has ever taken an active part in University affairs.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_06.png

name: McMurchy, Jean
motto: "She has a Will of her own."
description: Miss McMurchy moved to North Dakota before she was a year old and has ever since been a resident of our state. Most of her elementary education she received in the public schools of Fargo. After attending the Fargo High School for two years she taught very successfully a term in her home school. She then came to the University, finished her preparatory work, and took up the Normal course. She is a very careful and painstaking student.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_06.png

name: McLean, Annie
motto: "Virtue only makes our bliss below, And all our knowledge is ourselves to know."
description: Miss McLean was born in Canada, but it did not take her long to discover the superior advantages of the country on this side of the line. Hence it is that she has been a citizen of our state the greater part of her life. Her preparatory work was done at the University. She has always been an enthusiastic participant in basket-ball. She does not make a fuss about every little thing but simply goes ahead and does her work.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Metzger, Mabel
motto: "Now, don't flatter yourself."
description: Miss Metzger was born in the metropolis of the Western continent. She knew, however, that North Dakota was by far a better place and so she came here. She attended the public school at Williston before coming to the University. To say that she is enthusiastic over basket-ball is not enough; she is unquestionably one of the best players among the girls. Miss Metzger is perfectly willing to let other people have their own opinions provided they follow the golden rule.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Olson, Dagney
high school: Bruflat Academy at Portland, N. D.
motto: "A woman good without pretense, Blessed with plain reason and sober sense."
description: She was born in Christiania, Norway, and there she received her elementary education in a private school for girls. After graduating from Bruflat Academy at Portland, N. D., she attended the State Normal at Mayville for two years. She entered the University in the fall of 1903 and immediately proved her ability as a student and a scholar.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Shue, Florence A.
motto: "There may be others, but—Oh, my!"
description: She was born at Lakota but later moved to Cooperstown, which is now her home. In 1903 she finished a four-year high school course at Beloit, Wis. She came to the University in the fall of the same year and immediately joined our Normal class. We like her well, but we should have liked her better had she not gone to Wisconsin for her high school preparation.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Swarstad, Helga
motto: "So well to know Her own, that what she wills to do or say Seems wisest, virtuest, discreetest, best."
hometown: Willmar, Minn., to Thompson, N. D.
description: She came from Willmar, Minn., to Thompson, N. D., where she finished her high school course. She entered the University in the fall of 1902, but stayed away last year and taught school. She is a local editor of the Weekly Student and secretary of Mimer. With her beaming countenance and cheerful smiles she spreads happiness wherever she goes.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Turner, Vida May
high school: Aberdeen High School, State Normal at Madison, S. D., State Normal at Mayville, N. D.
motto: "She of the western dome, whose weighty sense Flows in fit words and heavenly eloquence."
description: She came to us from South Dakota, which is the state of her birth. She attended the Aberdeen High School one year, the State Normal at Madison, S. D., one year, and the State Normal at Mayville, N. D., one year. Last year she came to the University and joined the Normal class of '05. She won first place in the Main declamation contest last year and this year she took part in the local oratorical contest. She is an associate editor of the Weekly Student.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Wehe, Maude Lucretia
motto: "Our wills and fates do so contrary run That our devices still are overthrown."
description: She is the only member of our class that can claim Chicago for her birthplace. She showed good sense when she came to Grand Forks, where her home is at present. She graduated from the Grand Forks High School with the class of '03, and entered the University in the fall of the same year. She will be a splendid teacher, for her kindness of heart is unsurpassed.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Wardrope, Maud
high school: country schools of her home, preparatory course at the University
motto: "I awoke one morning and found myself famous."
description: Miss Wardrope was born in Marquette, Mich. Her parents came to North Dakota and settled at Churchs Ferry when she was one year old. Her early education was received in the country schools of her home. She finished the preparatory course at the University and then joined the Normal class. Last year she taught school for nine months. She will, therefore, have experience as well as theory when she graduates. This year she was a member of the A. D. T. team in the Gansl medal debate.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Hamilton, Helen N.
motto: "She with all the charm of woman, She with all the breadth of man."
description: Because of her scholarly ability in the classroom and her charming personality at all times, she has become known as the "law school's pride," this girl from old Kentucky. To the men of the class no prophet's vision of the future of this Portia of today could be too full of promise. Whether she pleads at the bar, presides on the bench, or graces a home, she will have a career no less enviable than that of the most successful member of the class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Andrews, R. M.
motto: "The public be damned."—Pullman.
description: Although perhaps not so outspoken as Mr. Pullman, Mr. Andrews, with true American spirit, generally says just what he thinks and always thinks just what he pleases. A varied experience linked together with a good deal of ambition, since his advent into the sea of human endeavor, which took place in Wisconsin, have resulted in the making of a "good man." Norwich, North Dakota, is his home.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Andrews, F. B.
motto: "Thou art a fellow of good respect."
description: One day back in the '70s a little fellow opened his eyes upon the light of day in the pine woods of Wisconsin. This was F. B. Andrews, the senior brother of "R. M." Since that day he has rambled and roamed over infinite stretches of territory and done everything from picking huckleberries on the hillslopes of Pennsylvania to the taming of the bronco on the Western plains. Seek his acquaintance, for his friendship is substantial, and look for him on the bench.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Bergland, J. J.
motto: "Youth hath the patience and the faith of Saints."
description: This mild-voiced philosopher is chiefly noted because of his superb contempt for detail and the tenacity with which he clings to a fundamental principle when he finds one. He first reached for the moon in Minnesota, and the added years have only increased the height of his ambition. His favorite pastime is taking bar examinations.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Bitzing, H. R.
motto: "I care not, Fortune, what you me deny—You cannot rob me of free nature's grace."
description: Somewhat good looking, with a calm and amiable disposition, this young man of sturdy German extraction came from St. Paul in the fall of 1904. He is a "Hoosier" by birth, a "Jay Hawker" by adoption and a "Flickertail" by choice. His accomplishments are many, in the inventory of which we find journalism, oratory and rare powers of winning feminine admiration.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Calder, W. L. A.
motto: "Bald was his head on the outside."
description: He is a native of Canada, but acquired his college education in North Dakota, and for several years wielded the pointer and ferrule in district schools of this state. He has seen a good many summers, has tasted the fruits of public office, and enjoyed the conveniences of married life. Though not so young and sprightly as some, his brain is just as active and has often been responsible for quotations of law that the judge sitting in Moot Court could not comprehend.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Chatfield, M. M.
motto: "Am I a coward? Who calls me villain? Breaks my pate across? Plucks off my beard?"
description: The only information obtainable concerning him was that he has lived in the state two years. His past—that is, if he has had a past—is wrapped in the darkest obscurity. As we know him, however, he is an athlete, a hard student and an altogether likable fellow. He is the happiest when carrying Miss Hamilton's books.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Connolly, L. H.
motto: "And the villain still pursued her."
description: This young man with soft brown eyes, gentle manner and no small degree of histrionic ability, hails from Mandan, N. D. He was born in St. Paul, Minn., but at a tender age took up his abode on the Missouri slope, where alkali dust and rigorous ranch duties contributed to the make-up of a real "puncher." Though styled, in his college career, a "maverick," he has, by his genial disposition, branded his friendship into the hearts of all his classmates. He will abandon the stage which has made him famous in such roles as "Uncle Jed," "Jerry, the Tramp" and "Alkali Ike, the One-Eyed Detective," and devote his time exclusively to the practice of the law.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Martin, O. Dahle
motto: "An excellent musician, and her hair shall be of what color it please God."
description: This ruddy descendant of Eric the Red entertains a reverential regard for his Norse ancestry and never misses an opportunity to proclaim it. He is an accomplished musician and a poet by nature, if not by reputation. Woman, however, he worships from afar, though he does not say that distance lends enchantment to the view. Iowa claims his birthplace, but he longs for the rock-bound coasts of Norway.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Devaney, H. J.
motto: "God made the Irish and they don't amount to much."
description: Although he is a native of Minnesota, and has wandered over the wind-swept plains of sunny Texas, he still insists that North Dakota is the belle of the states. He is a logical speaker and represented his Alma Mater on the recent Manitoba debate. He has been with the University seven years, completing both the arts and law courses. He is especially fond of railroading.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Enge, R. S.
high school: Highland Park College, at Des Moines
motto: "I have no spur to prick the sides of my intent."
description: Mr. Enge is a native of Iowa, having been educated in the Highland Park College, at Des Moines. He entered the U. N. D. in the autumn of '04. He is proud of his Teutonic origin, but often regrets that his Viking ancestry were compelled to wield the oar to accomplish the perpetuation of the race. "Elbert Hubbard struck me when he wrote the following," quotes Mr. Enge: "It wearies me to take a walk, for when I move around, I always have to lift my foot and put it on the ground."
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Germain, E. D.
motto: "Would that he were fatter."
description: He was born and raised in Wisconsin, but for some time prior to his launching into a legal career was engaged in the art of photography in the Mouse River country. Mr. Germain is one of the few married men in the class. His keen powers of legal persuasion and cross examination will, we predict, gain for him a prominent place with the future practitioners of North Dakota.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Hagen, C. C.
motto: "Good fellowship is the ship for me."
description: He is a true-blue type of the good fellow, because it is in him and because of his wide experience with men, having traveled a good many years as salesman and representative for some of the leading machine companies in the West. Sherbrooke is his domicile.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Houska, Chas.
motto: "That raspy flute; that quinsied flute!"
description: As his football playing is only excelled by his ability on the baseball diamond, and this in turn is surpassed only by his grace in dancing, is it any great wonder then that he is a favorite with the maidens? He was born in this state of an ancestry who lived on the plains of Bohemia. His college life was passed in Minnesota. Houska is in the zenith of his glory when riding on the top of a big red band wagon and coaxing such sweet strains from his clarionet as to be the envy and admiration of the small boy.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Larson, Fred
motto: "Love hath his seat in reason and is judicious."
description: Debater, gallant and savant, all of these and much more is this affable son of North Dakota. It is true he belongs to the Bachelors' Club, but he is the horrible warning and not the meritorious example. Bar examinations have no terrors for him, as evidenced by the fact of his recent purchase of a large law library. Grand Forks will be the arena in which his forensic powers will be exercised.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Leverson, Oliver
motto: "I beg of you, my dear Gaston."
description: A more courteous, generous and open-hearted gentleman would be hard to find. He was born in Iowa, and does not hesitate to say that the ending of his name is no exception to the rule. Aside from being the owner of a large rural estate in Minnesota, which is his present home, he is endowed with many other good qualities that are especially attractive to the ladies, the principal one of which is graceful dancing.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Martineau, L. L.
motto: "Let his youthfulness be no hindrance to his being reverently esteemed."
description: A typical French gentleman he is, with mild voice and charming manner, both of which have won him many friends. He was educated in St. Boniface and St. Thomas Colleges in Manitoba and St. Paul, respectively. He takes his greatest pleasure in strolling with high school girls.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Maxfield, Homer J.
motto: "And on their own merits modest men are dumb."
description: Our class historian, the subject of this sketch, assigned to a humbler pen the pleasure of its writing. In ancient times seven Grecian cities contended for the honor of Homer's birthplace, but he solemnly assures us that to Springfield, Ill., belongs that proud distinction. For five years, however, North Dakota has claimed him as her own. He first entered the law school in 1902, and after a year's absence, cast his lot with the present class. The favorite amusement of this genial, robust gentleman is to pick the mandolin and hum the popular airs.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_13.png

name: McDonald, George A.
motto: "Good things are done up in small packages."
description: "Mac" is an old-timer at the "U," having obtained his B. A. in '99. Though light in avoirdupois, he is a heavy weight in contests involving the exercise of gray matter. This highland laddie is well known in federal court circles as "the law student who swung the jury in the personal injury case." Foot racing and jig dancing constitute his favorite diversions.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Scherer, John D.
motto: "Still waters run deep."
description: A Minnesota product by birth and education, he is a striking example of Gopher civilization. Though he is mild and unobtrusive in manner, yet his quiet bearing conceals a purpose to do and be. He is especially proud of the little son who lords it over his household.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Snyder, N. F.
motto: "Woman! why hast thou thus ensnared my soul and body?"
description: Mr. Snyder is young in years but old in experience. The gray hairs on his youthful pate are suggestive of intense early reflections and indicative of the power of a massive brain, even though perched on a frail physique. His initiation into the busy world took place in Wisconsin, but for many years he has made his home at St. Johns, N. D.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Stafne, Albert J.
motto: "No man's life, liberty or property is safe when the legislature is in session."
description: Mr. Stafne comes from Abercrombie, N. D., having been born and raised in that section of the country. His two aims in life are to be in the legislature and own a bank. If good looks and feminine popularity help, he will accomplish both.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Totten, E. P.
motto: "It is better to be right than president."
description: Born in England, but at an early age manifesting a desire to abandon English soil without relinquishing native eccentricities, he came across and has been in North Dakota twelve years, six of which were spent in Fargo College. He is a Democrat of the old school, a staunch exponent of the Henry George economic doctrines and a good judge of feminine beauty. The theater and dance are his favorite amusements, for there it is that woman appears at her best.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Wardrope, Victor
motto: "Face that was fair and good to look upon."
description: For three seasons this doughty athlete has captained a victorious football team, and in many a game the tide has been turned in favor of the U. N. D. by his brilliant rushes and tackles. In the realms of debate he has also won honors for his Alma Mater. It is with woman's heart, however, he has created his greatest havoc, as no girl can long withstand his charm of manner. Ontario was honored by his birth but his daily excursions to Davis Hall and the Cottage have made of him a typical American.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Viaux, Victor
motto: "Of thy unspoken word thou art master, thy spoken word is master of thee."
description: In this fragment of human clay rests the acme of New England education, culture and refinement. He is a Yankee gentleman, a strict disciplinarian and a connoisseur of Boston baked beans. North Dakota might well be proud to call him her own, but his love for the law of his native commonwealth, where the tenure of judicial office is made to depend upon good behavior, as well as a lofty personal esteem for "Joe" Beale, will, no doubt, turn his steps toward Yankee soil where the "prodigal calf" will be slain and the fatted son rejoiced.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Wingerd, H. B.
motto: "There is a good deal of oratory in me, but I don't do as well as I can, out of respect to the memory of Patrick Henry."
description: Born in Pennsylvania, for some years and now a resident of Iowa. His education was obtained in the Keystone State Normal, Pennsylvania, and Highland Park College, Des Moines, Iowa. Mr. Wingerd knows a thing or two about politics and Poland China hogs. His motto, "Get on the band wagon if you want to enjoy political honors," will no doubt find for him a place in the Republican ranks of big men.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Carr, Jean
motto: "A rosebud set with little wilful thorns."
description: Jean, our witty girl, claims Minot as her home. Although she is small in stature, she makes up for it by her happy smile, which always raises the temperature of the atmosphere about her by several degrees. Her two aims in life are: First, to debate the question of "Woman's Rights;" second, to realize her ideal—six feet four.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Anderson, John M.
motto: "Bid me discourse, I will enchant thy ear."
description: John Anderson, our Jo John, is a graduate of the Valley City Normal; entered the University as a Sophomore last year. While at Valley City he made an excellent record as a debater and an orator, and he has not, as yet, broken this record. He has been on two Intersociety debating teams, and on the victorious Intercollegiate debating team against the "U" of Manitoba, '04; winner of first place in the state oratorical contest, '05.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Boise, David W.
motto: "In he comes, one broad substantial smile."
description: Dave is one of the pioneer members of our class, having joined us as a Freshman from the U. N. D. preparatory department. He is prominent on the basket-ball and baseball field; chief promoter of Dacotah; member of 'Varsity Book Store firm. A favorite among the young ladies, and always in good humor.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Bull, Daniel F.
motto: "Sport that wrinkled care derides And laughter holding both his sides."
description: This enthusiastic tennis player came to us from Newburgh (N. Y.) High School. His keen sense of humor always evinces itself when he hears a good story. Is noted for wearing a small blue cap, and winning a place in the Main declamation contest last spring.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Collinson, Mary
motto: "Of softest manner, unaffected mind, Lover of peace and friend of human kind."
description: Mary joined us in her Sophomore year, having taken her Freshman work at the University of Minnesota. You might call her little in stature, but she is big in brains, heart and ambition. She is typical of the girls of '06, being both a painstaking student and a jolly companion.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_17.png

name: Burtness, Olger B.
motto: "Honor, worth and courage, these indeed His sustenance and birthright are."
description: Because of his mighty muscle, Mr. Burtness has won fame as a football player, and because of his mighty voice, he has won fame as a debater. He has been a participant in three Intersociety debates and two Intercollegiate debates. Won Thomas medal in 1901. He is one of the young men through whose efforts this great annual was made possible.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_17.png

name: Comfort, Arthur B.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: "Kim," our pretty boy, is a prominent member of the Glee Club and the 'Varsity band. His laugh is as rosy as his cheeks and he is a living example of what a good complexion can do for a man. He is a persistent worker, faithful and enthusiastic. His one failing is any good looking girl.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_17.png

name: Conmy, John P.
motto: "Ever walking, ever talking, Not with many, but with one."
description: "Chick" spent his Freshman and Sophomore years at the University, but left us last fall to enter St. Thomas College, St. Paul. He, however, decided that no class could compare with the class of '06, and returned at the beginning of the winter term. He is an all-round athlete, conspicuous on the football and baseball fields, and in track work. Fond of country walking.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_17.png

name: De Kay, Eugenia
motto: "Her air, her manner, all who saw admired; Courteous, tho' coy, gentle tho' retired."
description: Eugenia comes from the Gopher State, but Grand Forks has been her home for the past four years. She is considered dignified by some, but those who really know her never make such a mistake. She bears the reputation of being one of the jolliest girls in her class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_18.png

name: Dolve, Nels O.
motto: "True as a needle to the pole; or dial to the sun."
description: Mr. Dolve came to us this year from Portland. He is a remarkably good student and has already proved himself worthy of belonging to the famous class of '06. We consider that he has shown good judgment in coming to the University, in view of the fact that he has brothers in various of our other state institutions.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_18.png

name: Hurd, A. W.
motto: "What sweet delight a quiet life affords."
description: Mr. Hurd was born in Dodgeville, Wis. He attended both the Lawrence University at Appleton, Wis., and Hamline before coming here. He established a reputation as a good worker in both and is keeping up that reputation here. Mr. Hurd's musical talent is of no mean worth. He shows signs of becoming a great artist in this line.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_18.png

name: Hyslop, Kenneth
motto: "What should a man do but be merry?"
description: Ken, our honored president, surnamed Hyslop, is a merry youth, and a friend of everyone. Ladies all like him—best proof of his ability. We all like him—best proof of his popularity.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_18.png

name: Flemington, Mary B.
motto: "So unaffected, so composed a mind, So firm, yet soft; so strong, yet so refined."
description: After completing her high school course, Mary took two years' work in the State Manual Training School, graduating there in 1903. She entered the "U" last year. It was her strong arm, guided by her keen eye, that broke the bottle of champagne over the prow of J. J. Hill's great steamship, The Dakota. She delights in physics and calculus and simply revels in analytics and such trifles. A true friend in time of need.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_19.png

name: Jackson, Bruce
motto: "His modesty's a candle to his merit."
description: Bruce represents the class of '06 in the study of the humanities, especially Greek and Latin. It is said he reads the Greek testament more readily than the English one. He is a declaimer of considerable ability and can make the "jassamine flower on her breast" look very attractive. He blushes almost as readily as a girl but we can easily forgive him that.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_19.png

name: Putnam, Grace B.
motto: "Her voice is ever soft, gentle and low, an excellent thing in a woman."
description: After her graduation from the Mayville Normal, Mrs. Putnam taught school for a few years. Last year she entered the "U" and graduated with the Normal class of '04. While here she was so favorably impressed with the Junior class that she decided to continue her studies with them. A better student it would be hard to find, conscientious, studious and exact.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_19.png

name: Johnson, John A.
motto: "A little nonsense, now and then, is relished by the wisest men."
description: Mr. Johnson has been so misjudged by some of us as to have been considered one of those quiet, bashful people who never play, or never understand a joke. But facts have come to light lately to wholly discredit such an idea. That his propensity for playing practical jokes has not been developed lately is assured, for when he was a boy he was the pest of the neighborhood. He is clever also in other lines. He came to us as a graduate of the Valley City Normal. He has taken part in one Intersociety and one Intercollegiate debate.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_19.png

name: Elsie, W. Mcfarland
motto: "She's modest as any, and blithe as she's bonnie."
description: Elsie graduated from the Valley City Normal last spring and entered the "U" this fall. She is our athletic girl. Last year she was captain of the Valley City Basket-ball team and this year she is holding down first base on the Girls' Indoor Baseball team. Though she has been with us but a year, her happy smile has made her a general favorite.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_20.png

name: Mark, Lovell
motto: "Talk to him of Jacob's ladder, And he would ask you, how many rounds."
description: Marcus Aurelius Pius Tullius claims direct lineage from Marcus Tullius Cicero. He loves mathematics and steam engines, is of a philosophic turn of mind, never lets himself be hurried, and is especially devoted to small girls.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_20.png

name: Fred, Mccurdy
motto: "Better to have loved a small girl than never to have loved 'a tall.'"
description: This son of Scotland has been with us since the beginning of the class. His first year at the University, he established a reputation as a "Plugger," but since he has developed a subtle and keener art, that of bluffing. His excuse is, of course, excessive athletic work, especially on the track. He has participated in two Intersociety debates and in one Intercollegiate debate. "Mac" is everybody's friend.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_20.png

name: Harry, Mcdonald
motto: "He is not short, he is not tall, He is the lad that courts them all."
description: Valley City High School gave "Mac" his start. He is a jolly good fellow, and a great friend of public amusements. Is known at the "U" as the "Dance Promoter." He is a man of consequence in athletics, playing on both the football and the basket-ball teams.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_20.png

name: Gretzsinger, Laura
motto: "A maid whose cheek outblooms the rose."
description: Laura is frankness personified. She never forgets to tell "the whole truth." We might call her either "our modest girl," or "our good-natured girl." She was never known to be angry and her sympathetic disposition has attracted many close friends to her.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_21.png

name: Richards, Ray
motto: "The light that lies in woman's eyes Has been my soul's undoing."
description: What would the institution do without "Old Pal"? He has a varied history behind him, but at present is becoming one of the requisites for running the institution. He has done valiant service on the U. S. G. S. He is now recognized as a general favorite among the "fairies," having recently dedicated a handsome memorial window in the Cottage. He is popular with the young men but more so with the young ladies. We hope he will never leave us.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_21.png

name: Walker, J. Douglas
motto: "On with the dance, let joy be unconfined."
description: It is said that the underlying incentive which brought Douglas here was his love for military tactics, and he has ever been an ardent devotee to that science. Perhaps this accounts for his military bearing. Last year he won fame by scaling the walls of Main building and gallantly defending the flag of '06. He is one of the "hustlers" of our class, and his efforts in making both the Junior Ball and the Junior Annual successful are worthy of mention.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_21.png

name: Sanderson, Frances
motto: "She goes the even tenor of her way."
description: Frances has won the enviable reputation of never letting herself be worried. It would take no less than a cyclone to tear her away from an interesting book, and the same force, only, could separate her from her friends. She is an enthusiastic member of Adelphi and has twice been chosen by her society to represent them in the Gansl medal debate.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_22.png

name: Webb, Arthur
motto: "He doth indeed show some sparks that are like wit."
description: "Art" is one of those white-robed angels that flit about the dining room. But this is not the only "wardrobe" he owns. He came to us last year from the southern part of the state where he had previously finished his preparatory work and a year of college. The northern surroundings have proved to hold attractions for him and it is rumored that the town where he hunts geese will be his future home.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_22.png

name: Westergaard, W. C.
motto: "Never idle a moment, but thrifty and thoughtful of others."
description: Mr. Westergaard graduated from the Valley City Normal in 1903, and joined our class this year. He has been on one of Adelphi's Intersociety debating teams, and, like all the students from Valley City, is a diligent and conscientious worker. The Dacotah has found Mr. Westergaard's literary and artistic abilities indispensable.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_22.png

name: Colborn, George O.
motto: "One of the few, the immortal names, That were not born to die."
description: "It is not good that man should be alone," therefore eighteen fanciful fairies have decided to keep him company in his noteworthy normal course. Poetically and musically inclined, he ever strives to attain his ideals: James Whitcomb Riley and Paderewski.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_23.png

name: Crary, Ethel M.
motto: "I have a heart with room for every joy."
description: Ethel was born at Crary, N. D., but for the past year her home has been at Grand Forks. She attended the Rockford High School, in Iowa, for one year, entering the preparatory department of the "U" in 1902. Though now residing in town, we still count her as one of the "Davis" girls. Her blithe and bonny presence is sadly missed, for she has a companionable and merry disposition.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_23.png

name: Carkin, Theone E.
motto: "Her modest looks the cottage might adorn Sweet as the primrose, peeps beneath the thorn."
description: She is pleasant, agreeable and cheering; but, above all, her sweetest charms lie in her unaffected modesty and the affectionate devotion with which she always regards her brother. She is a good student and a diligent worker.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_23.png

name: Fiering, Ida A.
motto: "The blushing beauties of a modest maid."
description: She was born in the Badger State, finished high school in the Gopher State, and is at present with the Normals of '06 in the Flickertail State. The third and last is always the best. She loves to ponder the deepest psychological problems. We wait to see the outcome.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_23.png

name: Hecker, Cora
motto: "Zealous, yet modest."
description: She graduated from the high school at Alden, Iowa, in 1900, and in the fall of the same year came west to her home at Minot. She performs all her duties with skill and fidelity, and in the classroom is not afraid to express her opinions, though she does so modestly.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_24.png

name: Helgesen, R. Marion
motto: "The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength and skill."
description: She entered the preparatory department of the University in 1901, and is now a member of the present class. She is one of the determined kind. Having a fixed purpose in mind, she is constant in pursuing that purpose, and therefore accomplishes whatever she undertakes to do.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_24.png

name: Hamel, Elizabeth Mae
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"
description: "Take things as they come" is this young lady's motto. She possesses that rare satisfaction "which holds the mind in peace, restraining complaint, opposition, or further desire." While at Grafton she was captain of the high school basket-ball team, and represented the Grafton High School in the declamation contest held at Grand Forks last fall.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_24.png

name: Henry, Mabel A.
motto: "The very room, coz she was in, Seemed warm from floor to ceilin'."
description: The girl with the raven hair and the light in her eye is one of the representatives of the Larimore High School. She is a good student and a conscientious worker, and takes an active interest in all University proceedings.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_24.png

name: Lund, Mabel E.
motto: "Pretty to walk with, Witty to talk with, And pleasant, too, think on."
description: Kindred has the distinction of sending her to us. After spending two years at Fargo High School, Mabel entered the 'U' last year, and completed her preparatory work. She has won the reputation of being one of the jolliest and best-natured girls in her class. She is noted for her artistic ability and also for being one of the few class historians who handed their material to the Dacotah on time, and the Dacotah board is grateful to her for it.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_25.png

name: Lykkens, Cora
motto: "Where duty leads, My course be onward still."
description: Miss Lykken comes from Grafton, and is a graduate of the Grafton High School. First cousin of Henry G., we are not surprised to learn that she, too, is of philosophic temperament. She is a diligent student, and at Grafton was salutatorian of her class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_25.png

name: McIntosh, Ellen E.
motto: "Wise to resolve and patient to perform."
clubs: Chairman of Devotional Com. Y. W. C. A.
description: She was born at Ormond, Ontario, but at a very early age moved with parents to Crystal, N. D., where she completed the first two years of her high school course. She entered the preparatory department of the University in 1900 and is at present a member of the Junior Normal class. Kind and generous, she is always ready to help others.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_25.png

name: McIntosh, Arnetta
motto: "Full of mirth and merriment."
description: She often gives vent to her pleasurable feelings, and when she laughs makes the world laugh with her. She graduated from the St. Thomas High School in '04, and came here, not only to become a member of our illustrious class, but also to help the baseball team of the 'Navy Blues' by her matchless pitching.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_25.png

name: Nicholson, Celia A.
motto: "True eloquence consists in saying all that is necessary, and nothing but what is necessary."
clubs: baseball girls in the "Pink and Green" team
description: She believes in making haste slowly, but always gets there on time.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_26.png

name: Poe, Lulu Leone
motto:
"For she was jes' the quiet kind
Whose natures never vary,
Like streams that keep a summer wind
Snow-hid in Jenoory."
description: If a "t" were added to the "Poe" she would be a poet in name as well as in fame. She is an excellent student and a faithful worker, and has that grace that wins all who see her.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_26.png

name: Radcliffe, Helen S.
motto: "Gentle of speech, beneficent of mind."
hometown: Morland, Ontario; later Inkster, North Dakota
description: Constant and steady, she believes in close application to business.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_26.png

name: Sprague, Clara E.
motto: "The mildest manners, and the gentlest heart."
description: This maid of the tranquil mind and the placid disposition comes to us from the Gopher State.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_26.png

name: Harriet, W. Wallace
motto: "A face with gladness overspread; Soft smiles, by human kindness bred."
description: Miss Wallace, after graduating from the Drayton High School, entered the "U" in the fall of 1903, and is now a member of the immortal Junior Normal class. She is captain of the baseball team of the "Blues" and has distinguished herself as catcher. Her dimpled cheek and smiling lips are visible signs of her cheerful disposition.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_27.png

name: Lena, J. Wilkinson
motto: "Within her tender eye The heaven of April with its changing light."
description: This young lady graduated from Devils Lake High School in 1903, and entered the University in the fall of 1904 as a Junior Normal. She has formed the habit of attention and devotion to her studies regardless of the fact that there are suburban attractions in existence.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_27.png

name: Clara, Wolff
motto: "Bright as the sun her eyes the gazers strike, And, like the sun, they shine on all alike."
description: "X" stands for "excellent"—so the story goes—and so this maid of virtue and wisdom is. She has the reputation of being not only the intellectual ray of her class, but also the musical ray of the institution, charming all by the soft, sweet strains of her "fiddle and bow."
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_27.png

name: Burke, Edward
motto: "Into this Universe, and why not knowing, Nor where, like water, willy-nilly flowing."
description: We know our latest acquisition best as "brother of Pat," well known at the "U" as a Celt of the most enthusiastic brand. To acknowledge the nationality of Ed is to admit that much can be made of even an Irishman if caught young.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_28.png

name: Christie, Gordon J.
motto: "East-side, west-side, all around the town," etc.
description: This specimen was rounded up at Notre Dame University and brought to our Law School to receive a few finishing touches. He is yet new and untried, but much is expected with good associations.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_28.png

name: Coulter, John Lee
motto: "Who mixed reason with stories And wisdom with jokes."
description: John utilizes every moment of his time and gets rest only by diversification. He is a graduate of the University, class of '04; is president of the Athletic Association, and was a member of the Forum debating team. He is also an authority on love, marriage and divorce; on the first, because of long practice in holding "his own," and on the latter because of original research work for his M. A. thesis at Minto and other places of holding court.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_28.png

name: Dieter, Charles V.
motto: "A real man encumbered with a boy's moustache."
description: Mr. Dieter was captured in the bad lands of Montana last fall and has since been tamed; he is now perfectly harmless. He seems to say "Shall I go on? Or, have I said enough?" In Dieter, the Hon. W. J. Bryan has found a worthy admirer and our classmate isn't ashamed to admit it. This Junior is studying to become a specialist in breach-of-promise suits.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_28.png

name: Foote, Leroy A.
motto: "Small bodies with velocity have a greater momentum than large ones without it."
clubs: Forum representative on the Weekly Student
Treasurer of the Law School Literary Society
description: He has won an enviable reputation. He is said to be in no way related to the man at the foot of the class, but is admitted to be a semi-relative of him who writes the foot-notes, and he is directly descended from Commodore Foote of Fort Donaldson fame. He is also one of the survivors of Custer's Indian massacre. Ask him about it.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_29.png

name: Graham, Fred J.
high school: Ellendale High School, State Manual Training School
description: Mr. Graham was born at Stockbridge, Mich., 1881. Early in life he showed a desire for better things and, when three years old, he took his father by the hand and led him to what is now LaMoure Co., in this state. He is a fine student, warm-hearted, sincere, modest, and a general favorite. His friends vote him a "Prince." His greatest fault is his love for the girls, but in this fault he is sincere and impartial.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_29.png

name: Grimson, Gudmunder
motto: "Oh, judge thou me by what I am."
clubs: Business manager of the Junior Annual
description: If Mundi (our international monstrosity) were twins, he could not do more work. He graduated from the "U" in 1904 as president of his class, and may well be proud of the reputation gained as business manager of the Junior Annual issued by that class. Now he is at once a graduate student at the "U;" a Junior law man; honorary member of Adelphi, and "our genial postmaster." Although a Bachelor of Arts and a member of the Bachelors' Club, Mundi has not escaped Cupid's darts and is probably as near the fatal precipice as man may safely approach.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_29.png

name: Greer, Truax W.
motto: "Some people never open their mouth without putting their foot in it."
description: Truax is our "much married man" from Sleepy Hollow. He, a pedagogue of no mean reputation, came from Grafton to swell our ranks. His extensive acquaintance with Shakespeare and women will be testified to by all summer school students. Some say that he is as regular as the most irregular man in the class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_29.png

name: Gulseth, Joseph
motto: "He always looks before he leaps."
description: Joseph is our class president and bears up under the burden as only a man used to the cares of a family could. Having wandered into Grand Forks and entered a "lost, strayed or stolen" ad in the want columns of a local paper, he was quickly taken in by our class. We knew a good thing when we saw it—and got it at a bargain.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_30.png

name: Gullickson, John A.
motto: "The simple, silent, selfless man is worth a world of tonguestress."
description: This geological specimen became dislodged from its moorings, was carried northwest by the powers that be, and, fortunately for himself and the Juniors, was rescued and is now safely anchored. John's strenuous experience as sergeant-at-arms will prove a fine training if he becomes a frontier politician as he plans.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_30.png

name: Langer, William
motto: "Fidelity is seven-tenths of business success."
description: William came to us from Casselton High School and was received with open arms by— He was at once groomed in the vice-president's toga and now holds down that position with all the dignity of a Fairbanks. Although never before away from home, (?) and never having seen a street car, (?) it is said he has not even gotten home-sick (?) nor did he get frightened at the car. (?)
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_30.png

name: Maloney, William
motto: "I would have married a long time ago, but I could not find one worthy of my worthy self."
description: Here is another of our Western captives. He has spent the last four years in the machine business. William joined the first law class in the "U" but soon left for a more remunerative business. Having made his "pile," he has again entered the legal profession and says he'll stay by it till the last cent is spent. If all reports are true, "The Subject" of this sketch is taking a double course in partnership—one in the law school and the other out on the "U" Campus (at least since the Celtic banquet).
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_30.png

name: Maloney, Charles F.
motto: "Are my gray hairs symbolical of piety or wisdom?"
description: Formerly a popular student here at the "U," Charles has been for the past four years a prospering business man in the Western part of the state. He is studying law with a purpose, but what that purpose is we have been unable to find out. Nevertheless, we will pass him in as a good fellow and as one worthy of success in whatever he undertakes to do.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_31.png

name: McMullen, Charles
motto: "He needs no eulogy, he can speak for himself."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, the Junior Class
Secretary and Treasurer, the G. F. H. S. Alumni
Secretary and Treasurer, the J. D. S. C.
description: Like Patrick Henry of Revolutionary fame, Charlie is said to have tried his hand at every department at the "U." And now like the same Patrick, he has finally settled down to the final choice—law. We also have it on good authority that he is a great favorite with Prexy at the "U."
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_31.png

name: Moseby, Samuel
motto: "Thy praise or dispraise are alike to me."
description: Our silver-tongued orator from St. Olaf's College spent one year "teaching the young idea how to shoot," in order to pass away the time until he could join the present Junior Class. Last fall he carried off first honors for the law school in the oratorical contest, and he certainly did the thing up in proper style. Strange stories are heard about him, and one night we heard him murmur about his "No. 22:" "So sweetly she bade me adieu I thought she bade me return." And we all believe him.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_31.png

name: Williams, John E.
motto: A man among a thousand—"girls," provided he found so many together at one time.
description: John is known as our Hercules, likewise as our Apollo.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1906.pdf_页面_31.png
